summary: Joel Spolsky announces Fog Creek Software's annual open house event, scheduled for July 17, 2008 at their Manhattan office. He notes that they normally decline tour requests because they prioritize giving programmers quiet working conditions. The company is about a month away from moving to a larger downtown space but chose to hold the event at the old office anyway. Visitors will get to meet interns, MBA program participants, sales staff, and developers working on FogBugz, Copilot, and Wasabi.
key_points: Fog Creek regularly declines office tour requests to protect programmers' quiet working conditions
The annual open house is the one exception to their no-tours policy
The company was preparing to move to a larger downtown office space
The event showcased their various teams including interns ('Dingos'), MBA participants ('SMTPs'), and product developers
money_quotes: Here at Fog Creek Software we get a lot of requests for a tour of the office, which we usually have to decline: we have this unusual obsession with giving programmers quiet working conditions.
Some kind of food-like snack will be served. Tiny cheddar-cheese-flavored crackers in the shape of fish, maybe. Don't skip lunch.
key_insight: Fog Creek's willingness to decline tour requests year-round reflects their commitment to prioritizing developer productivity over PR opportunities, with the annual open house as a carefully bounded exception.
